reaches.name: Truckee River at Derby Dam (closed 1,000 ft below dam)
Truckee River — E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) downstream to Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Truckee River: Open year around except for closed areas. Daily: 3 game fish except in that section from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) downstream to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary, limit is three game fish of which 1 may be trout. Possession: 6 game fish except in that section from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) downstream to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary, limit is 6 game fish of which 2 may be trout. CLOSED WATERS: Truckee River from Derby Dam downstream 1,000 feet.

sources.title: Truckee River — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Rainbow trout, Brown trout, Cutthroat trout (Lahontan species), Mountain whitefish, Largemouth bass (lower sections only).

sources.title: Nevada Western Region Fishing Regulations (eRegulations digest of NDOW rules)
sources.quotedText: In the stretch from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) to Derby Dam and from 1,000 feet downstream of Derby Dam to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary: only artificial lures with single barbless hooks may be used.

sources.title: Fish Catch Limit Changed for Truckee River — 2News
sources.quotedText: Only artificial lures with single barbless hooks may be used in the Truckee River (Washoe and Storey County) in that portion from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) to Derby Dam and from 1,000 feet downstream of Derby Dam to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary.

parameters.note: Open year-round except for closed waters (see closure rule); fishing hours any hour of the day or night.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe/Storey County, Truckee River
regulations.humanSummary: Truckee River (NV) is open to fishing year-round, any hour of the day or night, except the closed area at Derby Dam.
regulations.verbatimText: Open year around except for closed areas.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies to the Truckee River generally (Washoe/Storey County), except the reach from E Mustang Road bridge downstream to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary, which carries a further trout sub-limit (see reach-scoped bag rule).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe/Storey County, Truckee River
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (species composition unrestricted) on the Truckee River (NV) outside the special reach below.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish except in that section from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) downstream to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary, limit is three game fish of which 1 may be trout. Possession: 6 game fish except in that section from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) downstream to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary, limit is 6 game fish of which 2 may be trout.

sub_limits.note: Of the daily/possession game-fish limit, only this many may be trout.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies in the reach from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) downstream to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe/Storey County, Truckee River (special reach)
regulations.humanSummary: In the reach from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) downstream to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary: daily limit 3 game fish of which only 1 may be trout; possession limit 6 game fish of which only 2 may be trout.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish except in that section from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) downstream to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary, limit is three game fish of which 1 may be trout. Possession: 6 game fish except in that section from E Mustang Road bridge (I-80 Exit 23) downstream to the Pyramid Lake Paiute Reservation boundary, limit is 6 game fish of which 2 may be trout.

parameters.note: Instituted around the Derby Dam fish-screen project to protect migrating Lahontan cutthroat trout; corroborated by two secondary sources but not independently confirmed by us in the primary CR 25-16 season table itself.

regulations.citation: NDOW regional regulations digest (eRegulations), corroborated by local news report
regulations.humanSummary: In this reach, only artificial lures with a single barbless hook may be used (no bait).
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with single barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: No fishing within 1,000 feet downstream of Derby Dam.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County closed waters
regulations.humanSummary: Truckee River closed to all fishing for 1,000 feet downstream of Derby Dam, year-round.
regulations.verbatimText: Truckee River from Derby Dam downstream 1,000 feet.

parameters.note: No minimum or maximum size limit found for trout on the Truckee River (Washoe/Storey County) in NDOW's current fishing-seasons regulation; absence of a size-limit row for this water in that document is treated as a verified absence, not an oversight.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe/Storey County (no size-limit row for Truckee River trout)
regulations.humanSummary: No minimum or maximum size limit for trout on the Truckee River (NV) — verified absence.
